an6.99:1.1:
1
"Truly, bhikkhus, (1) it is impossible that a bhikkhu who considers any conditioned phenomenon to be pleasurable will possess a conviction in conformity
. (2) It is impossible that one who does not possess a conviction in conformity
will enter upon the fixed course of rightness. (3) It is impossible that one who does not enter upon the fixed course of rightness will realize the fruit of stream-entry, (4) the fruit of once-returning, (5) the fruit of non-returning, (6) or arahantship.
an6.99:1.2:
"Bhikkhus, (1) it is possible that a bhikkhu who considers all conditioned phenomena to be suffering will possess a conviction in conformity
. (2) It is possible that one who possesses a conviction in conformity
will enter upon the fixed course of rightness. (3) It is possible that one who enters upon the fixed course of rightness will realize the fruit of stream-entry, (4) the fruit of once-returning, (5) the fruit of non-returning, (6) or arahantship."
